protocol.purpose: Ensure each agent operates within a bounded workspace, preventing cross-agent file collisions and enforcing explicit artifact handoffs.

rules.rule: Each agent activation receives a workspace root directory scoped to its current workflow step.
Agents may read shared artifact directories (docs/planning-artifacts/, docs/implementation-artifacts/) but must not write to another agent's workspace.
Cross-agent file access requires an explicit handoff entry in the workflow's output manifest.
On workflow completion, the gate-enforcer verifies all workspace artifacts are accounted for — committed, handed off, or explicitly discarded.
Orphaned workspaces older than the configured retention period are flagged by memory-hygiene protocol for cleanup.

handoff-protocol.step: Producer agent writes artifact to its workspace.
Producer records handoff in checkpoint: artifact path, target agent, sha256 hash.
Consumer agent receives handoff reference via workflow-engine context.

integration.protocol: Workspace state is included in checkpoint snapshots.
Orphaned workspaces are cleaned on the configured cadence.
Post-complete gate verifies workspace artifact accounting.
